content: Mem-D V1 is a local-first read-only CLI analyzer.
Mem-D V1 answers what is inside agent memory and how redundant it is.
Mem-D V1 does not modify memory.
Mem-D V1 does not store memory.
Mem-D V1 does not retrieve memory.
The Mem-D pipeline starts with Parser.
The Mem-D pipeline runs Normalization after Parser.
The Mem-D pipeline runs Categorization before Embeddings.
The Mem-D pipeline runs Similarity after Embeddings.
The Mem-D pipeline runs Clustering after Similarity.
The Mem-D pipeline runs Metrics after Clustering.
The Mem-D pipeline ends with Report and CLI output.
memd/pipeline.py orchestrates analyze_file().
memd/parser outputs MemoryRecord objects.
memd/normalization.py cleans MemoryRecord objects.
memd/categorization.py outputs CategorizedMemory objects.
memd/embeddings.py outputs EmbeddedMemory objects.
memd/similarity.py computes cosine similarity.
memd/clustering.py outputs DuplicateCluster objects.
memd/reports.py renders terminal, JSON, and Markdown reports.
Mem-D CLI uses Typer.
Mem-D validation uses Pydantic v2.
Mem-D clustering uses scikit-learn DBSCAN.
Mem-D terminal UI uses Rich.
Mem-D tests use pytest.
Mem-D linting uses ruff.
MemoryRecord requires id and content.
CategorizedMemory contains memoryId, category, and confidence.
Mem-D categories include Preference, Fact, Task, Goal, Relationship, Temporary, and Unknown.
Similarity and confidence must be normalized between 0 and 1.
Compression opportunity equals duplicate percentage in V1.
The user wants the Obsidian vault excluded from the GitHub repo.
The user planned to publish Mem-D to a private GitHub repository before polishing it.
The user wants governance recommendations to be evidence-backed.
The user does not want execution, mutation, deletion, or memory rewriting in governance planning.
The FoMO study examined undergraduate medical and dental students in Peshawar.
The FoMO study used a cross-sectional design.
The FoMO study included 335 respondents.
The FoMO study sample was 58.2% male.
The FoMO study sample was 41.8% female.
MBBS students represented about 57.3% of the FoMO sample.
BDS students represented about 42.7% of the FoMO sample.
The FoMO study reported 58.8% day scholars.
The FoMO study reported 41.2% hostelites.
The FoMO study reported 9.9% substance use.
The mean age in the FoMO study was 21.79 years.
Age was not significantly associated with FoMO in the study.
Monthly household income was significantly associated with FoMO group membership.
High FoMO students reported mean monthly income of PKR 241,316.
Low FoMO students reported mean monthly income of PKR 200,395.
The overall prevalence of high FoMO was 23.6%.
The overall prevalence of low FoMO was 76.4%.
MBBS students had 26.6% high FoMO prevalence.
BDS students had 19.6% high FoMO prevalence.
Overall gender association with FoMO was not statistically significant.
Among MBBS students, gender and FoMO were significantly associated.
Among BDS students, gender and FoMO were not significantly associated.
Living arrangement was not significantly associated with FoMO overall.
Screen time was significantly associated with FoMO overall.
Heavy MBBS screen users had 32.8% high FoMO.
Screen time was not significantly associated with FoMO among BDS students.
Severe social media addiction prevalence was 23.9%.
Severe social media addiction was strongly associated with High FoMO.
Among students with severe social media addiction, 85% had High FoMO.
Among students with low-to-moderate addiction, 4.3% had High FoMO.
The FoMO study interprets social media addiction and FoMO as bidirectional.
About 43% of FoMO study participants reported feeling somewhat to very lonely.
Students with higher loneliness also had significantly higher FoMO.
Prof. Dr. Farhana Jabeen Shah supervised the FoMO project.
Malka I Saba was project director of the FoMO project.
Muhammad Tufail was assistant director of the FoMO project.
Yami was finance director of the FoMO project.
The normal distribution assignment assumes mean income of $3,000.
The normal distribution assignment assumes standard deviation of $500.
The Z-score for $2,500 income is -1.00.
P(X < $2,500) was calculated as 0.1587.
The Z-score for $3,500 income is +1.00.
A family earning $3,500 was placed at about the 84th percentile.
Simple random sampling gives every population member an equal chance of selection.
Stratified sampling divides a population into subgroups before sampling.
There are 11 families with more than four members in the sampling assignment.
The cumulative frequency problem says 12 students spent between 10 and 15 hours.
The probability assignment defines sample space as all possible outcomes.
The die roll sample space is {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}.
The coin-flip-and-die-roll sample space has 12 outcomes.
Rolling even and rolling odd on the same die roll are mutually exclusive.
The leadership and team building survey included 150 students.
Both Leadership and Team Building had 50 students.
With replacement, P(green then yellow) is 2/9.
Without replacement, P(green then yellow) is 8/33.
BIC-Z2F says time management is a must.
BIC-Z2F says to gather information about potential clients or investors.
BIC-Z2F recommends smartly using AI for research and quick brainstorming.
BIC-Z2F todo includes customer persona and journey mapping.
The BIC-Z2F MVP includes QR, dashboard, onboarding, and SMS API.
The BIC-Z2F product should have customizable QR codes for each business.
The BIC-Z2F pitch deck should include a monthly recurring revenue model.
The BIC-Z2F pitch deck should include future phases for scaling.
The user wants prompts that make generated content interactive.
The user has an idea for an all-in-one dashboard for offline AIs.
The user has an idea for a game about entering each other's dreams.
The user has an idea for a Sanderlings running from ocean game.
The user has an idea for a noise cancellation patch near or behind the ears.
The user has an idea called EYEZ that gives realtime vision to AI agents.
The user has an idea for a colony game.
The user has an idea for a sleep mask with slight vibrations for better sleep.
The user wants extreme automated storytelling for their brand.
The user has an idea called The Plank running Game.
The user has an idea called HumanNLoop.
HumanNLoop means getting humans in the agentic loop.
HumanNLoop would send notifications after step results for approval or disapproval.
The user thinks HumanNLoop is good enough for Y Combinator.
The user thinks Pinterest ads may be best for US audience and surgical niches.
The user has an idea for AI-only understandable gibberish language.
The AI-only language idea aims to keep prompt data safe from exposure.
The user has an idea for a tower defense game with ancient civilization wars.
The user has an idea for an AR plant game with Pokemon-like plants.
Flyingship game has placeholder sounds in UI and combat.
Flyingship character scenes need their own character PNGs.
Flyingship task says to check the latest guide for implementation and testing.
The user is considering process automation with LLM at MAADI.
The user is considering large language models at MAADI.
The user is considering computer vision at State Technical University MAADI.
The user is considering junior data analyst.
The stock price assignment created a Java program for 10 days of stock prices.
The stock assignment uses both an array and an ArrayList.
The stock assignment calculates average stock price.
The stock assignment finds maximum stock price.
The stock assignment counts occurrences of a specific price.
The stock assignment computes cumulative sum of prices.
The target stock price is 102.0.
The stock array contains 104.0.
The CS2205 assignment is about XML, JSON, namespaces, and data interchange.
The CS2205 XSD schema defines a bookstore catalog.
The XSD root element is catalog.
Each book has an id attribute and an available attribute.
The title element contains a publicationYear sub-element.
The author element contains firstName and lastName.
The HTML bookstore inventory uses a JSON object called inventory.
The inventory JSON stores three books.
The inventory includes The Great Gatsby.
The Great Gatsby author is F. Scott Fitzgerald.
The inventory includes To Kill a Mockingbird.
To Kill a Mockingbird author is Harper Lee.
The inventory includes 1984.
1984 author is George Orwell.
1984 is marked unavailable.
XML namespaces prevent naming conflicts.
XML supports schemas, attributes, and namespaces.
JSON is lightweight and maps directly to JavaScript objects.
